messages.content: Oh good, that's a relief. Um, one more thing though. I was wondering like, should I be avoiding any foods or drinks while I'm on this medication? I've heard potassium can be a problem with some blood pressure meds.
messages.content_redacted: Oh good, that's a relief. Um, one more thing though. I was wondering like, should I be avoiding any foods or drinks while I'm on this medication? I've heard potassium can be a problem with some blood pressure meds.

messages.content: That's actually a really insightful question. With ACE inhibitors like Lisinopril, you do need to be careful about your potassium intake because the medication can cause your body to retain potassium. So you should avoid potassium supplements and try to limit high potassium foods like bananas, oranges, tomatoes, and spinach. Just eat them in moderation, you don't have to eliminate them completely. And definitely avoid salt substitutes as those are usually high in potassium.

messages.content: So moderate alcohol consumption is generally okay, but you want to be careful because alcohol can enhance the blood pressure lowering effect of Lisinopril. So if you do drink, keep it to one drink per day if you're a woman, or up to two if you're a man, and make sure you're staying hydrated. The combination could make you feel more dizzy, so just be aware of that, especially if you're already experiencing some dizziness from starting the medication.

messages.content: I totally understand your concern, and I appreciate you bringing that up. messages.content: Good questions. So for the expedited renewal, the total cost is one hundred thirty dollars. That's one hundred dollars for the passport book renewal itself, plus the thirty dollar execution fee, and then the sixty dollar expedited service fee. As for passport photos, yes, there are specific requirements. The photo needs to be two by two inches, taken within the last six months, and it has to be a color photo with a white background. Your face should take up about fifty percent of the photo, and you need to have a neutral expression with your mouth closed. Most pharmacies like Walgreens or CVS can take passport photos, or you can have them taken at the acceptance facility itself.
